authoritative_sources.title: California Film & Television Tax Credit Program 4.0 Program Guidelines
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The California Film Commission officially administers the California production incentive program. Its program guidelines are authoritative for production cost-report packaging, qualified expenditure treatment, CPA review, and final allocation workflow.

authoritative_sources.title: California Film & Television Tax Credit Program 4.0 Agreed-Upon Procedures
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The official California agreed-upon-procedures work program is a direct production-accounting workflow reference for what a CPA must test, including production reports, call sheets, timecards, payroll reports, invoices, petty cash, and ledger support.
used_for: timecard, payroll, petty-cash, and invoice evidence expectations

authoritative_sources.title: California Film & Television Tax Credit Program 4.0 Qualified Expenditure Chart
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The official California qualified expenditure chart is a direct classification and control reference for above-the-line and below-the-line wages, fringes, non-qualified costs, and exclusions that production accounting cannot infer safely.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Cost classification and incentive support review

authoritative_sources.title: Film Tax Audit Procedures Manual
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Georgia DOR administers a major production incentive program and its audit manual is authoritative for payroll support, loan-out support, contractor validation, sales-tax handling, cost-ledger evidence, and audit workflow packaging.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Production accounting audit support and closeout

authoritative_sources.title: Film Tax Credit Resources
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Georgia DOR's official resources page is authoritative for current audit forms, vendor guidance, loan-out withholding posture, and verification-of-in-state-work support used in production-accounting documentation.

authoritative_sources.title: Theatrical Getting Started
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: SAG-AFTRA is the official performers' union and this signatory guidance is authoritative for performer production paperwork, Exhibit G reporting, payroll services, and pension and health contribution obligations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Cast payroll setup and signatory support review
used_for: Exhibit G and cast paperwork requirements

authoritative_sources.title: Basic Agreement
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The DGA is the official guild for directors, assistant directors, and unit production managers. Its Basic Agreement is authoritative for role classifications and labor-cost obligations that shape production-accounting evidence and escalation paths.
used_for: unit production manager and AD classification context

authoritative_sources.title: Rate Cards 2025 - 2026
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Current DGA rate cards are an official compensation reference across theatrical, television, and network categories, making them directly relevant to broadcast versus film versus streaming labor-cost support and rate-sensitive discrepancy review.

authoritative_sources.title: 2024 Area Standards Agreement Memorandum of Agreement
authoritative_sources.publisher: Alliance of Motion Picture and Television Producers
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: AMPTP is an official party to IATSE film and television agreements. The current Area Standards Agreement materials are authoritative for crew wage, rest, meal-penalty, and benefit-obligation inputs that materially change payroll and fringe accounting.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Crew payroll and fringe support review
used_for: meal and rest penalty evidence boundaries

workflow_stages.name: Production setup and prerequisite validation
workflow_stages.description: Identify the production type, jurisdiction, signatory package, payroll-service model, incentive-program election, and system-of-record ownership that govern the case before evaluating the financial outcome.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Source-record and posting support review
workflow_stages.description: Validate that deal memos, timecards, Exhibit G reports, payroll registers, AP support, petty cash, DPRs, call sheets, and approvals support the requested posting, adjustment, or cost-report inclusion.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Payroll, petty cash, AP, and cost-report reconciliation
workflow_stages.description: Compare production paperwork, payroll, AP, bank, and cost-ledger records to identify matched items, unexplained variances, unsupported adjustments, and next owners.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Qualified-spend support and closeout packaging
workflow_stages.description: Prepare evidence-backed support for incentive ledgers, qualified-spend schedules, and closeout packets without certifying the claim or tax outcome.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Boundary and authority escalation
workflow_stages.description: Escalate when the work requires controller, payroll-tax, labor-relations, legal or business-affairs, residuals, or incentive-certification authority, or when signatory and incentive rules materially conflict.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

artifacts: approved production budgets, hot costs, cost reports, and account coding maps
crew and cast deal memos, start packets, timecards, and Exhibit G performer reports
payroll registers, fringe reports, loan-out support, and payroll-service exception reports
purchase orders, AP vouchers, invoices, check requests, petty-cash envelopes, and p-card logs
daily production reports, call sheets, travel support, location logs, and departmental approvals
cash books, bank statements, wire or ACH confirmations, and reconciliation workpapers
qualified-spend ledgers, in-state verification support, and incentive audit packets
tenant signatory packages, approval matrices, chart-of-accounts maps, and close checklists
systems_of_record: production accounting ledger and cost-reporting system
production payroll processor or payroll service
accounts payable, purchase order, and expense platforms
document repository for deal memos, timecards, call sheets, and audit support
budgeting, scheduling, and daily production reporting tools
rights-holder or studio billing system when billing context is in scope

allowed: Assess whether visible production source records support a posting, cost-report inclusion, reconciliation note, incentive-support package, or exception escalation.
Summarize matched and unmatched evidence, missing prerequisites, and the next responsible owner.
Separate broadcast, feature, episodic, live, remote, and streaming paths when the governing signatory or incentive context clearly requires different evidence.
escalate: The applicable production type, signatory package, incentive program, or system-of-record ownership is missing or conflicts with the public baseline.
Payroll, AP, bank, petty-cash, or cost-ledger records do not reconcile to one another.
A variance or override request requires controller, payroll-tax, labor-relations, legal, residuals, or incentive-certification authority.
Broadcast and scripted production contexts are blended even though the governing records, pay rules, or billing paths differ materially.
refuse: Requests to alter timecards, Exhibit G reports, payroll, AP, petty-cash, or cost-ledger facts without source evidence.
Requests to certify tax credits, decide payroll-tax or labor-contract interpretation, approve accounting policy, or provide legal advice.
Requests to treat the broadcast, film, and streaming industry label as cosmetic and skip production type, signatory, or incentive context.

domain_failure_modes: A cost is treated as supportable even though the underlying deal memo, timecard, DPR, invoice, or approval trail is incomplete or contradictory.
An unsupported reclassification is made to force a cost report or incentive ledger to tie before review.
Broadcast, scripted, and live-remote production workflows are flattened into one evidence model even though the governing records and labor obligations differ materially.
Loan-out, fringe, travel, or qualified-spend support is advanced without the location, withholding, or signatory evidence that the incentive program requires.
The lane drifts into controller approval, payroll-tax judgment, labor-relations interpretation, legal advice, or incentive-certification authority.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Production-type and signatory variance across live broadcast, scripted film or television, commercials, and streamer-first productions
unresolved_ambiguities.detail: The public baseline proves that production type and signatory package materially change payroll, paperwork, and cost-support rules. Tenant retrieval is still required before final handling because the exact agreement stack, payroll service, and billing path vary by production model.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: State-specific incentive treatment outside the California and Georgia public baseline
unresolved_ambiguities.detail: This pack uses California and Georgia as authoritative public incentive baselines because they publish detailed workflow and evidence materials. Tenant retrieval is required when another state, province, or country incentive program materially changes qualified-spend or audit expectations.

notes: The source mix covers workflow, evidence, terminology, and failure handling rather than only one dimension.

No blocking ambiguity remains for validated packaging because tenant-specific production model, signatory stack, and incentive election details are explicit prerequisites instead of hidden assumptions.
